senators.name: Mark Warner
senators.nerdReason: B.A. from George Washington University and J.D. from Harvard Law School. Made billions as a telecommunications entrepreneur and venture capitalist (co-founded Nextel). Former governor of Virginia. Vice Chairman of the Senate Intelligence Committee — one of the most analytically demanding positions in Congress. Combines elite legal education, technology entrepreneurship, and deep intelligence policy expertise.

senators.name: Tim Kaine
senators.nerdReason: B.A. from University of Missouri and J.D. from Harvard Law School. Career as civil rights attorney, Richmond mayor, and Virginia governor. 2016 Democratic VP nominee. Deep policy expertise on foreign affairs, armed services, and education. Harvard Law training and decades of detailed legislative work demonstrate methodical, analytical governance approach.

VA-01.name: Rob Wittman
VA-01.nerdReason: B.A. from Virginia Tech, M.A. from University of North Carolina, and Ph.D. in public policy from Virginia Commonwealth University. A doctorate demonstrates deep research methodology and analytical rigor. Career as shellfish sanitation biologist and environmental health specialist. Applies policy research expertise to defense and natural resources policy as Vice Chairman of the House Armed Services Committee.
education: Ph.D. Public Policy, Virginia Commonwealth University
experience: Vice Chairman, House Armed Services Committee

VA-02.name: Jen Kiggans
VA-02.nerdReason: B.S. from U.S. Naval Academy and M.S. in nursing (nurse practitioner). Navy helicopter pilot with 10 years of active duty service. Combines military analytical training from USNA, aviation expertise, and clinical healthcare credentials. Applies medical and military analytical skills to defense and veterans policy.

VA-03.name: Bobby Scott
VA-03.nerdReason: B.A. from Harvard University and J.D. from Boston College Law School. Longest-serving member of Virginia's delegation. Career as attorney and state legislator. Ranking Member of the House Education and Workforce Committee — deep expertise in education policy grounded in research and data. Known for meticulous legislative work on criminal justice reform and education equity.
experience: Ranking Member, House Education and Workforce Committee

VA-04.name: Jennifer McClellan
VA-04.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Richmond and J.D. from University of Virginia School of Law. Career as attorney and state legislator. UVA Law provides strong analytical training. Deep policy expertise on energy, environment, and voting rights. Solid credentials but approach not distinctly evidence-based beyond standard legal/legislative work.
education: J.D., University of Virginia School of Law

VA-05.name: John McGuire
VA-05.notNerdReason: Navy SEAL and Green Beret. Career as military special operations and state delegate. Military special operations require tactical analytical skills but no academic credentials or evidence-based policy approach in legislative record.

VA-06.name: Ben Cline
VA-06.notNerdReason: B.A. from Bates College and J.D. from University of Richmond School of Law. Career as attorney and state delegate. Legal training provides analytical skills but governance approach driven by conservative ideology rather than evidence-based analysis.
education: J.D., University of Richmond School of Law

VA-07.name: Eugene Vindman
VA-07.nerdReason: B.A. from SUNY Binghamton, M.A. from Central Michigan University, and J.D. from University of Georgia School of Law. Three post-secondary degrees. 25-year Army career as infantry officer, paratrooper (82nd Airborne), and JAG officer. Served as White House NSC deputy legal advisor. Combines military analytical discipline, legal expertise, and international law specialization. Came to the U.S. as a refugee from Soviet Ukraine.
education: J.D., University of Georgia School of Law
experience: White House NSC Deputy Legal Advisor

VA-08.name: Don Beyer
VA-08.nerdReason: B.A. magna cum laude in economics from Williams College (Phi Beta Kappa, Presidential Scholar). U.S. Ambassador to Switzerland and Liechtenstein. Known in Congress as a passionate advocate for STEM education, climate science, and data-driven policy. Has reportedly taken MIT online courses to deepen scientific literacy. Consistently cites economic research and climate data in legislative arguments. Williams Phi Beta Kappa credentials demonstrate exceptional academic achievement.
education: B.A. Economics (magna cum laude, Phi Beta Kappa), Williams College

VA-09.name: Morgan Griffith
VA-09.notNerdReason: B.A. from Emory and Henry College and J.D. from Washington and Lee University School of Law. Career as attorney and Virginia House of Delegates majority leader. Legal training provides analytical skills but governance approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: J.D., Washington and Lee University School of Law
experience: Virginia House of Delegates Majority Leader

VA-10.name: Suhas Subramanyam
VA-10.nerdReason: B.A. from Tulane University and J.D. from Northwestern University Pritzker School of Law. White House technology policy advisor under President Obama, leading task forces on IT modernization, job creation, and emerging technology regulation. Deep expertise in technology policy grounded in hands-on White House experience. Applied tech policy analysis in Virginia state legislature before Congress.
education: J.D., Northwestern University Pritzker School of Law
experience: White House Technology Policy Advisor (Obama administration)

VA-11.name: James Walkinshaw
VA-11.notNerdReason: B.A. in politics from New York University. Career as congressional chief of staff and county supervisor. Long experience in legislative operations but approach is more politically operational than analytically rigorous.
experience: Chief of Staff to Rep. Gerry Connolly
